description: The failed/cancelled coding-task visibility cleanup is tracked as its own harness task so future sessions can resume from the new Telegram task-selection rules without re-deriving the visibility policy.
steps: Open PLAN.json and verify it only tracks the failed/cancelled visibility cleanup harness.
Open PROGRESS.md and verify the reboot entry records the current baseline: failed tasks still block new `/coding` starts and `/coding list` still shows failed/cancelled tasks.

Confirm the scope remains limited to Telegram `/coding` orchestration, active-task semantics, task-list visibility, and documentation rather than changing persistence formats or deleting task history.

description: Workspace-wide and per-origin active-task selection treat only `starting`, `running`, and `waiting_user` as active so failed/cancelled tasks no longer block new `/coding` starts.
steps: Create failed and cancelled tasks and verify the workspace active-task selector ignores them.
Verify a running or waiting task still blocks new `/coding <repo> <goal>` creation.
Verify a failed task in the same Telegram chat no longer occupies the active slot used by default control selection.

description: Telegram `/coding list` hides failed/cancelled tasks and renumbers the visible list newest-first with 1-based indexes.
steps: Create running, completed, failed, and cancelled tasks for one Telegram private chat and verify `/coding list` only shows the visible tasks newest-first.
Verify failed and cancelled tasks remain persisted in the store and artifacts even though they disappear from Telegram list output.
Verify the empty-state message is clear when a chat only has hidden terminal tasks.

description: Slash index selection and default Telegram control targeting operate only on the visible task set, so hidden failed/cancelled tasks can no longer be targeted or chosen implicitly.
steps: Verify `/coding status 1`, `/coding pause 1`, `/coding resume 1`, and `/coding stop 1` target the first visible task rather than a hidden failed/cancelled task.
Verify out-of-range indexes return a clear error after visibility filtering.
Verify `/coding status` without an index returns a no-manageable-task message when a chat only has hidden terminal tasks.

description: Focused tests cover the new active-slot semantics, hidden list behavior, filtered index selection, and the unchanged ability to inspect hidden tasks directly by task id through CLI tooling.
steps: Run focused pytest for coding-task routing, policy, and manager behavior touching failed/cancelled visibility and selection.
Verify tests cover new `/coding list` hiding behavior and that failed tasks no longer block new starts.
Confirm `.venv/bin/python -m compileall` still succeeds for the touched coding-task modules and tests.

description: README and PROGRESS.md document that failed/cancelled coding tasks are hidden from Telegram `/coding` task management without being deleted from persisted history.
steps: Open README.md and verify it documents the hidden-terminal-task behavior for `/coding list` and active-slot selection.
Open PROGRESS.md and verify it records the baseline, the no-delete decision, and the completed implementation summary.
Confirm PLAN.json is fully marked complete after the implementation and verification pass.
